# VER-QUIC-CRT-0038 - QUIC first client-side 0-RTT packet-emission attempt verification

## Scope

Verify that the first client-side 0-RTT packet-emission attempt is admitted only when the dormant detached carrier has both the resumption credential material and the early-data prerequisite material, that the runtime derives distinct 0-RTT packet-protection material for the first protected client application packet, that missing prerequisites or derivation failures fall back cleanly, and that 0-RTT receive handling, anti-replay, key update, transfer/retry, and public API widening remain out of scope.

## Verification Method

Focused requirement-home tests, fuzzing, and BenchmarkDotNet evidence.

## Preconditions

- The repository builds successfully on net10.0.
- REQ-QUIC-CRT-0137, REQ-QUIC-CRT-0139, and REQ-QUIC-CRT-0140 remain landed prerequisites.
- REQ-QUIC-CRT-0141 remains the active first 0-RTT send-attempt requirement.
- A permanent BenchmarkDotNet suite exists under benchmarks/README.md for the 0-RTT send hot path.

## Procedure or Approach

- Inspect the dormant carrier, send gate, packet-protection derivation, and protected-packet assembly to confirm the attempt is allowed only when both prerequisite ingredients are present.
- Verify that the new 0-RTT packet-protection package is distinct from the 1-RTT package and that missing prerequisites or failed derivation leave the send gate closed.
- Run the focused requirement-home tests for positive first-send admission, negative fallback, and the public no-broad-support guard.
- Run fuzz coverage against the packet/protection boundary and the protected-packet formatter.
- Run the permanent BenchmarkDotNet suite, then the current CRT/API/INT guard set, the full `REQ_QUIC_CRT_` sweep, and repo-local SpecTrace render and validation checks.

## Expected Result

The managed client/runtime path can attempt the first bounded 0-RTT packet emission only when the dormant carrier is eligible, uses distinct 0-RTT packet-protection material, falls back cleanly when prerequisites are absent, and does not widen receive, anti-replay, key update, transfer, retry, or public support claims.

landed

## Acceptance Criteria

- The first client-side 0-RTT packet-emission attempt is only admitted when both the resumption credential material and the early-data prerequisite material are present.
- The 0-RTT packet-protection material is distinct from the current 1-RTT material.
- Missing prerequisites or failed derivation keep the send gate closed.
- The slice does not claim 0-RTT receive handling, anti-replay, key update, transfer, retry, or any public API widening.

## Benchmark Scope

- Add a permanent `QuicTlsClientZeroRttEmissionBenchmarks` suite under `benchmarks/` for the 0-RTT send hot path.
- Keep the benchmark focused on packet-protection derivation and protected packet formatting, not receive or replay control.
